name: Grok 4 Heavy
archDisplay: Grok 4 base model served via parallel multi-agent test-time compute (reports describe 8-32 agents cross-checking answers)
note: Multi-agent 'Heavy' configuration of Grok 4, consumer-only via the $300/month SuperGrok Heavy tier; no public API model slug.

results.notes: FrontierMath Tier 4 (v1, old), Epoch AI's independent evaluation (±2.1% CI) -- verified directly on Epoch's Grok 4 Heavy model page. This is a genuinely hard, unfavorable data point (in sharp contrast to the near-saturating academic benchmarks xAI itself publicized for this model), which is exactly the kind of non-cherry-picked coverage this pass is meant to surface.

Humanity's Last Exam, text-only subset, w/ Python + Internet tools -- distinct from the full (multimodal) set w/ tools score of 44.4 already recorded above. Confirmed verbatim on xAI's own Grok 4 launch page (x.ai/news/grok-4, fetched directly): 'Grok 4 Heavy saturates most academic benchmarks and is the first model to score 50.7% on Humanity's Last Exam, a benchmark designed to be the final closed-ended academic benchmark of its kind,' with the same 50.7% figure shown in the page's text-only-subset chart. Independently corroborated by DataCamp's Grok 4 benchmark table, which lists 'Grok 4 Heavy (with tools): 50.7%' for HLE.

A harder, less-favorable reasoning eval than the near-saturating math/science benchmarks already on file. Recorded separately from base Grok 4's own arc-agi-2 result (15.9, in corpus/models/spacexai/grok-4.json, sourced from introl.com and matching the wording on xAI's own launch page) because at least two press outlets explicitly attribute a distinct, higher 16.2% specifically to the Grok 4 Heavy configuration: TheTechPortal ('under the ARC-AGI-2 test... Grok 4 Heavy scored 16.2%, nearly double the score of the next best-performing commercial AI system') and DataStudios ('Grok 4 Heavy reached state-of-the-art 16.2%, roughly twice the next best model (Claude 4)'). Several more outlets (dig.watch, DataPhoenix, Blockchain Council) report the same 16.2% figure but attribute it more loosely to 'Grok 4' without specifying which variant. xAI's own launch page displays this metric only as an interactive, unlabeled bar chart (no scraped alt text), so the exact variant behind each number could not be confirmed at the primary source -- flagging this genuine ambiguity rather than silently picking one reading. Included because the recurrence of the specific '16.2' figure (not a plausible rounding of 15.9) across five independently-published outlets suggests it reflects a real, distinct data point from xAI's livestream materials, not reporting noise.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: xAI shipped Grok 4 (incl. the Heavy multi-agent config, gated behind the $300/mo SuperGrok Heavy tier) on 2025-07-09, corroborated by x.ai and multiple independent outlets. All 5 original result rows confirmed verbatim on their cited pages: HLE 44.4% appears literally on the Scientific American article ('That jumped to 44.4 percent with a version called Grok 4 Heavy'); GPQA 88.9%, AIME25 100.0%, LiveCodeBench 79.4%, and USAMO25 61.9% all appear literally in DataCamp's Grok 4 benchmark table. Confirmed via search that Grok 4 Heavy remains consumer-only (no API slug) and Grok 4 carries a 256K context window. paramsB/activeParamsB correctly left null — xAI has not disclosed them. Note xAI has since shipped newer Grok 4.3/4.20/4.5 variants, consistent with the 'deprecated' status.

Coverage-completeness pass (2026-07-14): fetched x.ai/news/grok-4 directly (Grok 4 Heavy is 'first to score 50.7% on Humanity's Last Exam (text-only subset)', added as a second hle row alongside the pre-existing full-set 44.4). Fetched DataCamp's full benchmark table (GPQA/AIME25/LCB/USAMO25 all reconfirmed against existing rows; HMMT25 96.7% could not be added, no taxonomy slug exists for it). Fetched Epoch AI's Grok 4 Heavy model page directly -- no additional textual benchmark data beyond the FrontierMath Tier 4 result already on file (the rest of the page is a JS-rendered dashboard). Added arc-agi-2 (16.2, independent/press-sourced, see notes on that row for the base-vs-Heavy attribution ambiguity). Investigated but explicitly did NOT add a SWE-bench Pro score: llmreference.com's Grok 4 Heavy page and its GPT-5.5-Pro-vs-Grok-4-Heavy comparison page both cite '39.8' sourced to Scale AI's public leaderboard, but this could not be corroborated -- Grok 4 Heavy does not appear at all on Scale's own live SWE-Bench Pro public leaderboard (fetched directly) nor on llm-stats.com's comprehensive 39-model SWE-Bench Pro tracker (fetched in full); rather than force an unverifiable number onto the record, this is flagged here as a known gap. Also checked and found no mappable Heavy-specific results for BrowseComp, GAIA, tau-bench, terminal-bench, BFCL, or MMLU-Pro -- press coverage of those either doesn't distinguish Heavy from base Grok 4, or doesn't cover Grok 4 Heavy at all. xAI never published a standalone, disambiguated Grok-4-Heavy-specific benchmark table beyond the STEM/math cluster and the two HLE rows already captured; the model is consumer-only (SuperGrok Heavy tier) and was never run through the third-party agentic/coding harnesses (SWE-bench, GAIA, tau-bench, etc.) that vendor-API-accessible models typically get evaluated on, which plausibly explains the thin harder-eval coverage relative to base Grok 4 and later Grok releases.
